statements.text: Tulips originated in Central Asia and were later cultivated in the Ottoman Empire.
Carolus Clusius introduced tulips to the Netherlands through the Leiden gardens.
Tulip Mania in the Netherlands drove the price of tulip bulbs to extraordinary levels.
The Bollenstreek in the Netherlands is famous for vast tulip fields that attract visitors each spring.
Keukenhof showcases diverse tulip displays and is central to the Netherlands' annual flower parades.
A breaking virus produced striped patterns in tulips such as Semper Augustus.
Tulips can self-pollinate, allowing some varieties to reproduce even without insect pollinators.
Carolus Clusius studied exotic plants and exchanged knowledge with botanists across Europe.
Tulip bulbs store nutrients that help the plant survive harsh winters in Central Asia.
During Tulip Mania, Semper Augustus became one of the most prized tulip bulbs in the Netherlands.
The Ottoman Empire valued tulips as symbols of beauty and status in court culture.
Interest in tulips at Leiden helped establish the Netherlands as a center for plant science.
